highlights: Boss Prep now includes a persisted Readiness Checklist per boss (Arena, Buffs, Summon, Mobility) with quick Mark Ready and clear actions in both Boss Tracker and Prep Guide.
Boss progression now extends into farming workflows with Drop Wishlist and Loot Tracking states (Wish/Got It), plus per-boss missing/acquired summaries and drop filter modes.
Item Lookup can now filter directly by tracked boss loot status, so wishlist and acquired drops are easy to review outside boss pages.
Loadout Builder now offers side-by-side comparison with URL-synced compare selections, metric deltas (Defense, Damage, Mobility, Summons, Utility), and slot-by-slot difference rows.
Saved search and filter presets now persist locally for Item Lookup, NPC Guide, and Biome Guide, including quick apply, rename, and delete controls.
Reset semantics and prep/drop UX were polished across tracker surfaces for faster repeated boss-attempt planning.
App release surfaces were bumped to 1.2.0 while keeping the Terraria data version fixed at 1.4.5.6.
Prefix compatibility coverage was expanded across the data model so Item Lookup can more accurately represent which modifiers apply to each item category.
Prefix preview behavior was tightened around the shared prefix logic and hook flow, improving tooltip accuracy when browsing candidate modifiers.
The prefix compatibility test suite was strengthened to lock the new matching rules in place and reduce regressions in future data updates.
App release surfaces were bumped to 1.1.1 while keeping the Terraria data version fixed at 1.4.5.6.
The Dungeon Defenders 2 crossover pass was extended with Etherian Mana plus the Gato, Dragon, and Creeper pet summons so Old One's Army rewards cover more of the remaining official crossover set.

Loadout Builder is now a first-class workflow with saved class loadouts, JSON import/export, URL-backed active selection, and immediate handoff from Item Lookup, Build Stages, and Boss Prep into the active loadout.
Prep Guide adds a dedicated boss-readiness route with class and phase filters, copyable per-boss or filtered prep summaries, summon requirements, danger windows, and execution notes in one view.

Boss prep cards now bridge directly into Build Stages, Loadouts, Boss Guide, and Item Lookup, including compact lookup links for the recommended weapon and armor so research can turn into a concrete gear setup without redoing filters.
App release surfaces were bumped to 1.1.0 while keeping the Terraria data version fixed at 1.4.5.6.
The next crossover release batch starts Dungeon Defenders 2 coverage with the Old One's Army foundation items: Defender Medal, Eternia Crystal, Eternia Crystal Stand, and Ale Tosser.
The first DD2 class-accessory wave is now modeled with Apprentice's Scarf and Squire's Shield from Dark Mage plus Huntress's Buckler and Monk's Belt from Ogre.
All four Tavernkeep sentry families are now represented across their Rod, Cane, and Staff tiers: Flameburst, Lightning Aura, Explosive Trap, and Ballista.
DD2 class loadouts now include the full Apprentice, Huntress, Monk, and Squire armor sets plus their post-Golem upgrades: Dark Artist, Red Riding, Shinobi Infiltrator, and Valhalla Knight.
Item Lookup now shows the DD2 sentry-family shimmer transmutation cycle for every rod, cane, and staff tier instead of only the Tavernkeep purchase unlocks.
The next smallest official crossover gap after DD2 was closed with One Drop's Code 2, keeping the 1.4.5.6 crossover pass incremental and current-version only.
App release surfaces were bumped to 1.0.4 while keeping the Terraria data version fixed at 1.4.5.6.
Biome Guide and NPC Guide now include copy-link actions with clearer share-state guidance for the current filtered view.
NPC Guide planner and biome preference filters persist in the URL for restorable shared setups.
App-version visibility and 404 recovery links were tightened across release-facing surfaces.

Item Lookup gained focused Don't Starve Together and Dead Cells crossover expansions, including Deer Thing support items, The Beheaded's vanity set, and exact Item Flask and Alchemy Flask crafting coverage.
Palworld coverage advanced incrementally with rescue-style acquisition notes for the Pal companions, exact Digtoise worldgen sourcing, Huge Dragon Egg opening outcomes, and the Pal Metal vanity set.
Base-material coverage was widened with Sand Block so Glass now has an exact upstream Furnace recipe instead of only downstream usage coverage.
The remaining single-item Core Keeper gap was closed with Glow Tulip and its exact world-generation sourcing.
Stardew Valley crossover coverage started with Joja Cola, Stardrop purification through the Dryad, and Blue Chicken Egg vendor coverage.
After the 1.4.4 and 1.4.5 crossover pass, the next smallest official gap was picked up with Minecraft's Creeper vanity set and its exact Halloween Goodie Bag sourcing.
The next smaller modern crossover bucket was completed with One Drop's yoyo line, including Rally, Code 1, Valor, Cascade, Format:C, Gradient, Chik, Yelets, and the exact Chik support recipe path.

Worms 3 was left out of the main dataset because its crossover items are legacy-only, so the current-version pass moved to Pixel Piracy with Falcon Blade, Pirate Staff, Ginger Beard, Pillagin' Me Pixels, and Sail.
The next official crossover gap was continued with Project Zomboid via Spiffo Plush and its exact zombie-family drop source.
Phone and tablet layouts were polished across Item Lookup, Build Stages, Boss Tracker, Biome Guide, and NPC Guide.
Shared UI controls now use roomier touch targets and more legible badges for non-desktop play.
Home, navigation, and README guidance were updated to better support local-network use on mobile and tablet devices.
Dedicated Biome Guide route added to main navigation and home discovery.
Biome Guide and NPC Guide now support shareable filtered views via URL-backed state.
NPC Guide simplified to NPC planning and preference workflows, with duplicate biome tab removed.
Boss gear references normalized to canonical item IDs with curated strategy sections.
Build Stages now cross-links into boss progression guides.
Item Lookup gained class/source/tier filters and side-by-side compare panel.
NPC Happiness Planner added for biome and roommate optimization.
Accessibility pass: high-contrast mode, reduced-motion mode, stronger focus visibility.
Expanded item and recipe dataset coverage.
Prefix compatibility validation improvements.
Boss tracker guide drawer quality improvements.
